tasks.title: API resolver utilities, Zod validation, and security hardening
tasks.description:
Extract shared resolver utilities and add input validation + security hardening across the API layer. This is a broad refactor touching most resolver files.

**1. Extract `requireTask` and `requireProject` utilities** — Create `apps/api/src/utils/resolverHelpers.ts` with two functions:
- `requireTask(context, taskId)`: fetches task with `include: { project: true }`, validates `task.orgId === user.orgId`, throws `NotFoundError` if missing or `AuthorizationError` if wrong org. Returns the task.
- `requireProject(context, projectId)`: fetches project, validates org ownership, returns project.
- Also extract `validateStatus(validStatuses: string[], proposed: string)` that throws `ValidationError` on mismatch — currently duplicated in `task.ts` lines 106-108 and 150-152.
- Replace all instances of the fetch+validate pattern across resolvers. The pattern appears 20+ times in: `task.ts` (lines 38-42, 144-148, 282-286, 319-324), `ai.ts` (lines 117-122, 165-170, 315-318, 354-361), `sprint.ts`, `github.ts`, etc. Import from the new helpers file.

**2. Add Zod input validation at resolver boundaries** — Add Zod schemas to the helpers file for key mutations:
- `createTask`: title max 500 chars, description max 10000 chars
- `updateTask`: same limits + URL format for any URL fields
- `createComment`: body max 5000 chars
- `createProject`: name max 200 chars
- Apply to `commitTaskPlan` — wrap the `Promise.all` task creation with proper error handling so individual failures don't silently swallow errors.

**3. Security hardening:**
- Sanitize `appendToTitles` in `previewTaskPlan` (ai.ts ~line 177-179) — user-provided titles are injected directly into the AI prompt without escaping. Escape quotes and wrap in safe delimiters.
- Add string length limits at resolver boundaries for task titles, descriptions, knowledge base content, and comments.
- Add rate limiting for password reset (`requestPasswordReset`) and email verification (`sendVerificationEmail`) endpoints — create an auth-specific rate limiter in `app.ts` similar to the existing `authLimiter` but targeting these mutations (5 req/min).
- Add Content-Security-Policy headers via helmet config in `app.ts` — currently using default helmet(). Add explicit CSP: `default-src 'self'`, `script-src 'self'`, `style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'`, `img-src 'self' data: https:`, `connect-src 'self'`.

**4. Add GraphQL error codes** — In the existing error classes (likely in `apps/api/src/graphql/errors.ts` or similar), add `extensions.code` fields: `ERR_NOT_FOUND`, `ERR_VALIDATION`, `ERR_UNAUTHORIZED`, `ERR_FORBIDDEN`, `ERR_RATE_LIMITED`. The graphql-yoga framework supports this via `GraphQLError` extensions. Update error throwing across resolvers to use these codes consistently.

Acceptance criteria:
- All resolver files use `requireTask`/`requireProject` instead of inline fetch+validate
- Zod validation runs on all create/update mutations with appropriate limits
- `appendToTitles` is sanitized before prompt injection
- `commitTaskPlan` handles individual task creation failures gracefully
- Helmet CSP is explicitly configured
- Password reset / email verification have dedicated rate limiting
- All GraphQL errors include a machine-readable `code` in extensions
- `pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build` all pass

tasks.title: Frontend cleanup — BacklogView decomposition, lazy-load markdown, setState refactor
tasks.description: Frontend architecture cleanup: decompose BacklogView, lazy-load heavy dependencies, and improve hook API design. **1. Decompose BacklogView.tsx** (~242 lines) — The backlog section (lines 172-214) follows the same pattern as the existing `SprintSection` component (lines 146-169). Extract a `BacklogSection` component that handles: - Checkbox header with select-all toggle - Drag-and-drop container with drop indicators - Task row rendering loop - The key difference from SprintSection is `sectionId: null` handling for unassigned tasks This should reduce BacklogView by ~40 lines and make the two section types consistent. **2. Lazy-load react-markdown** — In `apps/web/src/components/shared/MarkdownRenderer.tsx`, `react-markdown` and `remark-gfm` are eagerly imported at the top (~60KB gzipped combined). These are only used in TaskDetailPanel (description, acceptance criteria, instructions) and report panels. Convert to lazy loading: ```tsx const ReactMarkdown = React.lazy(() => import('react-markdown')); ``` Wrap usage in `Loading...}>`. Also lazy-load `remark-gfm` — since it's a plugin passed to ReactMarkdown, you may need a wrapper component that imports both lazily. **3. Replace direct setState injection in useSprintManagement** — Currently `useSprintManagement` (line 8) accepts `setTasks: React.Dispatch>` as a parameter and calls it directly to mutate task state (lines 53, 64). Instead, expose action functions that enforce invariants: - Replace `setTasks` parameter with a callback like `onTasksChanged: (updater: (tasks: Task[]) => Task[]) => void` - This way the hook doesn't have direct access to the raw setState, and the parent can enforce any invariants before applying the update - Update `useProjectData.ts` to pass the wrapped callback instead of raw `setTasks` Acceptance criteria: - BacklogView uses a `BacklogSection` component consistent with `SprintSection` - react-markdown + remark-gfm are lazy-loaded with Suspense fallback - useSprintManagement uses action callbacks instead of raw setState - No regressions in BacklogView DnD, markdown rendering, or sprint management - `pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build` all pass

tasks.title: Slack integration — full vertical slice with incoming webhooks and slash commands
tasks.description:
Add Slack integration for task event notifications and task creation from Slack. This is a full vertical slice: database model, backend service, GraphQL API, REST endpoints, and frontend settings UI.

**1. Prisma Model** — Create `apps/api/prisma/schema/slack.prisma`:
```prisma
model SlackIntegration {
  id            String   @id @default(uuid())
  orgId         String   @map("org_id")
  teamId        String   @map("team_id")
  teamName      String   @map("team_name")
  webhookUrl    String   @map("webhook_url")
  channelId     String   @map("channel_id")
  channelName   String   @map("channel_name")
  events        String   @default("[]")
  enabled       Boolean  @default(true)
  createdAt     DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  org           Org      @relation(fields: [orgId], references: [orgId])
  @@index([orgId])
  @@map("slack_integrations")
}
```
Add the `slackIntegrations` relation to `Org` in `org.prisma`. Run `npx prisma migrate dev --name add-slack-integration`.

**2. Slack Client Service** — Create `apps/api/src/slack/slackClient.ts`:
- `sendSlackNotification(webhookUrl, message)`: POST to Slack incoming webhook with formatted message blocks
- `formatTaskEvent(event, data)`: Convert task/sprint/comment events into Slack Block Kit messages with:
  - Task title as bold header, status badge, assignee mention
  - Link to task in TaskToad (construct from `CORS_ORIGINS` or a new `APP_URL` env var)
  - Color-coded attachment sidebar (green=created, blue=updated, red=deleted)
- Support same event types as webhooks: `task.created`, `task.updated`, `task.deleted`, `sprint.created`, `sprint.closed`, `comment.created`

**3. Notification Integration** — Create or update `apps/api/src/utils/notificationUtils.ts` with a `dispatchSlackNotifications(prisma, orgId, event, data)` function similar to `dispatchWebhooks`. Call it alongside webhook dispatch in task/sprint/comment resolvers. Use fire-and-forget (don't await, catch errors silently).

**4. Slash Command Endpoint** — Add `POST /api/slack/commands` in `app.ts`:
- Verify Slack request signature (HMAC SHA256 with signing secret from env `SLACK_SIGNING_SECRET`)
- Parse `/tasktoad create <title>` command → create task in the org's first project (or a configured default project)
- Return immediate Slack response with created task details
- This is a REST endpoint, not GraphQL, because Slack sends form-encoded POST data

**5. GraphQL API** — Create `apps/api/src/graphql/typedefs/slack.ts` and `resolvers/slack.ts`:
- Type: `SlackIntegration { id, teamId, teamName, channelId, channelName, events, enabled, createdAt }`
- Query: `slackIntegrations: [SlackIntegration!]!` (org-scoped)
- Mutations: `connectSlack(webhookUrl, teamId, teamName, channelId, channelName, events)`, `updateSlackIntegration(id, events?, enabled?)`, `disconnectSlack(id)`, `testSlackIntegration(id)`
- Register types and resolvers in `schema.ts`

**6. Frontend UI** — Create `apps/web/src/components/SlackSettings.tsx` and add a "Slack" section in `OrgSettings.tsx` (between the GitHub and Webhooks sections, around line 365):
- Show connected integrations with channel name, team name, enabled toggle
- Event checkboxes (same 6 events as webhooks)
- "Connect Slack" button that opens a form for webhook URL + channel details
- Test button to send a test notification
- Disconnect button with confirmation

Acceptance criteria:
- SlackIntegration model created with migration
- Slack notifications fire on task/sprint/comment events (same triggers as webhooks)
- Slash command endpoint creates tasks from Slack
- OrgSettings shows Slack section with full CRUD
- Test button sends a formatted test message to the configured channel
- `pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build` all pass
tasks.reviewNotes: Typecheck fails: slackIntegration does not exist on PrismaClient. You added slack.prisma schema but did NOT include a migration. Run: cd apps/api && npx prisma migrate dev --name add-slack-integration. The migration SQL file must be committed. Also fix: notificationUtils.ts lines 34 and 44 have implicit any types - add explicit types for the filter/map callback parameters.

tasks.title: Timeline/Gantt view and Portfolio overview page
tasks.description:
Add two new views: a Timeline/Gantt chart for individual projects and a Portfolio overview page for cross-project visibility.

**1. GanttChart Component** — Create `apps/web/src/components/GanttChart.tsx`:
- SVG-based horizontal timeline showing tasks as horizontal bars positioned by their `dueDate` and `estimatedHours`
- Each task bar: colored by status (use existing `status.*` Tailwind tokens), shows task title truncated inside the bar
- Dependency arrows: draw SVG arrows from parent task to dependent tasks using the `dependsOn` field (array of taskIds). Use curved paths (quadratic bezier) for cleaner visuals
- Left sidebar: task names list (fixed, ~200px) with task type badges (epic/story/task)
- Timeline header: date columns with day/week/month granularity. Default to week view
- Zoom controls: buttons to switch between day/week/month granularity
- Horizontal scroll for the timeline area (left sidebar stays fixed)
- Vertical scroll synced between sidebar and timeline
- Tasks without `dueDate` shown in a "No date" section at the bottom
- Click on a task bar calls `onSelectTask(taskId)` to open TaskDetailPanel
- Props: `tasks: Task[]`, `onSelectTask: (taskId: string) => void`
- Use the same pattern as BurndownChart.tsx for SVG rendering with ResizeObserver for responsiveness

**2. Integrate Gantt in ProjectDetail** — Add "Timeline" as a new view tab in ProjectDetail.tsx:
- Add to the view toggle buttons (around line 249-267) between Calendar and Dashboard
- Render `<GanttChart>` in the content area conditional block (around line 586+)
- Pass `filteredTasks` and `onSelectTask` handler

**3. Portfolio Overview Page** — Create `apps/web/src/pages/Portfolio.tsx`:
- New page at route `/app/portfolio` — add to `App.tsx` routes
- Add "Portfolio" link to the app navigation (wherever Projects link lives)
- Backend: Add `portfolioOverview` query to `typedefs/project.ts` and `resolvers/project.ts`:
  ```graphql
  type ProjectSummary {
    projectId: String!
    name: String!
    totalTasks: Int!
    completedTasks: Int!
    overdueTasks: Int!
    completionPercent: Float!
    activeSprint: String
    healthScore: Int
  }
  portfolioOverview: [ProjectSummary!]!
  ```
- Resolver aggregates across all user's org projects using Prisma `groupBy` or counts
- Frontend: Grid of project cards showing:
  - Project name (linked to project detail)
  - Circular progress indicator (completion %)
  - Health score badge (green >70, yellow 40-70, red <40)
  - Task counts (total, completed, overdue)
  - Active sprint name if any
  - Compact bar chart of status distribution

Acceptance criteria:
- GanttChart renders tasks as horizontal bars on a timeline with dependency arrows
- Zoom controls switch between day/week/month granularity
- Timeline tab appears in ProjectDetail and works with filtered tasks
- Portfolio page shows all projects with aggregated metrics
- `portfolioOverview` query returns correct counts
- Navigation includes Portfolio link
- `pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build` all pass

tasks.title: AI prompt history and historical trend analysis
tasks.description:
Add AI prompt replay/history for debugging and cost tracking, plus historical trend analysis over persisted reports.

**1. AIPromptLog Model** — Add to `apps/api/prisma/schema/aiusage.prisma`:
```prisma
model AIPromptLog {
  id           String   @id @default(uuid())
  orgId        String   @map("org_id")
  userId       String   @map("user_id")
  feature      String
  taskId       String?  @map("task_id")
  projectId    String?  @map("project_id")
  input        String   @db.Text
  output       String   @db.Text
  inputTokens  Int      @map("input_tokens")
  outputTokens Int      @map("output_tokens")
  costUSD      Float    @map("cost_usd")
  latencyMs    Int      @map("latency_ms")
  model        String
  cached       Boolean  @default(false)
  createdAt    DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  @@index([orgId, createdAt])
  @@index([taskId])
  @@index([projectId, createdAt])
  @@map("ai_prompt_logs")
}
```
Run `npx prisma migrate dev --name add-ai-prompt-log`.

**2. Persist prompts in AI client** — In `apps/api/src/ai/aiClient.ts`, after each successful AI call, persist the prompt log. The `callAI` function (or equivalent) already has access to input/output/tokens/cost/latency. Add a Prisma create call to `AIPromptLog` with the relevant context (feature, taskId, projectId passed through from the caller). Make this fire-and-forget (don't await, catch errors silently) to avoid slowing down AI responses.

**3. GraphQL API for prompt history** — Add to `typedefs/ai.ts`:
```graphql
type AIPromptLog {
  id: String!
  feature: String!
  taskId: String
  projectId: String
  input: String!
  output: String!
  inputTokens: Int!
  outputTokens: Int!
  costUSD: Float!
  latencyMs: Int!
  model: String!
  cached: Boolean!
  createdAt: String!
}
```
Query: `aiPromptHistory(taskId: String, projectId: String, limit: Int): [AIPromptLog!]!`
Resolver in `ai.ts`: query `AIPromptLog` filtered by taskId or projectId, ordered by createdAt desc, default limit 20.

**4. Frontend: TaskAIHistory** — Create `apps/web/src/components/taskdetail/TaskAIHistory.tsx`:
- Collapsible accordion section in TaskDetailPanel showing AI prompt history for the current task
- Each entry: feature name badge, timestamp, token count, cost, latency
- Click to expand: shows full prompt (input) and response (output) in an expandable section
- Use monospace font for prompt/response text
- Add this section to `TaskDetailPanel.tsx` after the Comments section

**5. Historical Trend Analysis** — Add `analyzeTrends` feature:
- Zod schema in `aiTypes.ts`: `TrendAnalysis { period, completionTrend, velocityTrend, healthTrend, insights: string[], recommendations: string[] }`
- Prompt builder in `promptBuilder.ts`: `buildTrendAnalysisPrompt(reports: Report[])` — takes last N persisted reports and asks AI to identify trends
- Service function in `aiService.ts`: `aiAnalyzeTrends(reports, config)`
- Config in `aiConfig.ts`: add `analyzeTrends` feature config (maxTokens, systemPrompt)
- GraphQL: `analyzeTrends(projectId: String!, period: String): TrendAnalysis!` query in `typedefs/ai.ts` and `resolvers/ai.ts`
- Resolver: fetch recent `Report` records for the project, pass to AI, return analysis

**6. Frontend: TrendAnalysisPanel** — Create `apps/web/src/components/TrendAnalysisPanel.tsx`:
- Panel similar to StandupReportPanel / ProjectHealthPanel
- Shows trend data: completion trend (up/down/stable with arrow), velocity trend, health trend
- Insights as bullet points, recommendations as action items
- Add "Trends" button to ProjectDetail toolbar (near Health/Standup buttons)

Acceptance criteria:
- AIPromptLog model created with migration
- All AI calls automatically persist prompt history
- `aiPromptHistory` query returns logs filtered by task or project
- TaskDetailPanel shows AI history section with expandable prompt/response
- `analyzeTrends` query analyzes historical reports and returns insights
- TrendAnalysisPanel renders trend data in ProjectDetail
- `pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build` all pass
tasks.reviewNotes: Merge conflict in apps/web/src/pages/ProjectDetail.tsx. Your branch includes task-004 changes that were already merged to main separately. Please rebase onto main (git rebase main) to pick up the merged task-004 code, then resolve the conflict in ProjectDetail.tsx. Also: you are missing a Prisma migration for the AIPromptLog model. Run: cd apps/api && npx prisma migrate dev --name add-ai-prompt-log. Commit the migration SQL file so the Prisma client can be generated during merge.
